---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis"
author: "Valiao, Syrelle Kaye D."
date: "2026-01-29"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo       = TRUE,
  warning    = FALSE,
  message    = FALSE,
  fig.align  = "center",
  fig.width  = 7,
  fig.height = 5,
  out.width  = "\\linewidth", 
  dpi        = 300,
  fig.pos    = "H"             
)
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(naniar)
library(patchwork)
library(corrplot)
library(ggridges)
library(viridis)
library(kableExtra)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(moments)   
library(dagitty)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r load-data}
analysis_data <- read_csv("analysis_data_final.csv")
```

## 1. Outcome Distribution (UIC)

```{r uic-filter}
# Restrict to observations with complete UIC
uic_data <- analysis_data %>% filter(!is.na(uic_raw))
cat("Sample size with complete UIC:", nrow(uic_data), "\n")
```

```{r uic-summary-raw}
# Summary statistics — raw UIC
uic_data %>%
  summarise(
    n        = n(),
    mean     = mean(uic_raw),
    sd       = sd(uic_raw),
    median   = median(uic_raw),
    q25      = quantile(uic_raw, 0.25),
    q75      = quantile(uic_raw, 0.75),
    min      = min(uic_raw),
    max      = max(uic_raw),
    cv       = sd(uic_raw) / mean(uic_raw),
    skewness = skewness(uic_raw),
    kurtosis = kurtosis(uic_raw)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Value = round(Value, 2)) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "UIC Summary Statistics")
```

```{r uic-summary-log}
# Summary statistics — log-transformed UIC
uic_data %>%
  summarise(
    n      = n(),
    mean   = mean(log_uic),
    sd     = sd(log_uic),
    median = median(log_uic),
    q25    = quantile(log_uic, 0.25),
    q75    = quantile(log_uic, 0.75),
    min    = min(log_uic),
    max    = max(log_uic)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Value = round(Value, 3)) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "Log(UIC) Summary Statistics")
```

```{r uic-who-classification}
# WHO iodine status classification
uic_categories <- uic_data %>%
  mutate(
    uic_category = case_when(
      uic_raw < 100                      ~ "Insufficient (<100)",
      uic_raw >= 100 & uic_raw < 200     ~ "Adequate (100-199)",
      uic_raw >= 200 & uic_raw < 300     ~ "Above requirements (200-299)",
      uic_raw >= 300                     ~ "Excessive (>=300)"
    ),
    uic_category = factor(uic_category,
                          levels = c("Insufficient (<100)",
                                     "Adequate (100-199)",
                                     "Above requirements (200-299)",
                                     "Excessive (>=300)"))
  )

uic_categories %>%
  count(uic_category) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
  kable(format = "simple",
        caption = "UIC Category Distribution (WHO thresholds)")
```

```{r uic-distribution-plots, fig.height=8}
# Four-panel distribution plot: raw vs log-transformed, histogram vs Q-Q
p1 <- ggplot(uic_data, aes(x = uic_raw)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 50,
                 fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(uic_data$uic_raw),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = median(uic_data$uic_raw), y = 0.003,
           label = paste("Median =", round(median(uic_data$uic_raw), 1)),
           hjust = -0.1, color = "darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Raw UIC",
       subtitle = paste0("n = ", nrow(uic_data)),
       x = "UIC (\u03bcg/L)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

p2 <- ggplot(uic_data, aes(x = log_uic)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 50,
                 fill = "coral", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_density(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(uic_data$log_uic),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = mean(uic_data$log_uic), y = 0.3,
           label = paste("Mean =", round(mean(uic_data$log_uic), 2)),
           hjust = -0.1, color = "darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Log(UIC)",
       subtitle = "More normally distributed",
       x = "Log(UIC)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

p3 <- ggplot(uic_data, aes(sample = uic_raw)) +
  stat_qq() + stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Q-Q Plot: Raw UIC",
       x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
  theme_minimal()

p4 <- ggplot(uic_data, aes(sample = log_uic)) +
  stat_qq() + stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Q-Q Plot: Log(UIC)",
       x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
  theme_minimal()

print((p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4))
```

## 2. Exposure Variation (Urban/Rural)

```{r urban-distribution}
# Overall urban/rural split
analysis_data %>%
  count(urban) %>%
  mutate(area_type = ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural"),
         percent   = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
  select(area_type, n, percent) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "Urban/Rural Distribution")
```

```{r uic-by-urban}
# UIC summary stratified by urbanicity
uic_data %>%
  group_by(urban) %>%
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw),
    sd_uic     = sd(uic_raw),
    median_uic = median(uic_raw),
    q25        = quantile(uic_raw, 0.25),
    q75        = quantile(uic_raw, 0.75),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(area_type = ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural"),
         across(where(is.numeric) & !c(urban, n), ~round(., 2))) %>%
  select(area_type, n, mean_uic, sd_uic, median_uic, q25, q75) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "UIC by Urban/Rural Status")
```

```{r urban-ttest}
# Two-sample t-test: urban vs rural UIC
t_test_result <- t.test(uic_raw ~ urban, data = uic_data)
cat("T-test (Urban vs Rural):",
    "\n  t        =", round(t_test_result$statistic, 3),
    "\n  p        =", format.pval(t_test_result$p.value, digits = 3),
    "\n  Mean diff=", round(diff(t_test_result$estimate), 2), "ug/L\n")
```

```{r urban-distribution-plots, fig.height=8}
# Density, boxplot, and violin plots by urbanicity
urban_colors    <- c("Rural" = "#E69F00", "Urban" = "#56B4E9")
uic_data_label  <- uic_data %>%
  mutate(area_type = factor(ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural")))

p6 <- ggplot(uic_data_label, aes(x = log_uic, fill = area_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = urban_colors) +
  labs(title = "UIC Distribution by Urbanization",
       x = "Log(UIC)", y = "Density", fill = "Area Type") +
  theme_minimal()

p7 <- ggplot(uic_data_label,
             aes(x = area_type, y = uic_raw, fill = area_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = urban_colors) +
  labs(title = "UIC by Urban/Rural Status",
       x = "Area Type", y = "UIC (\u03bcg/L, log scale)") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none")

p8 <- ggplot(uic_data_label,
             aes(x = area_type, y = log_uic, fill = area_type)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = urban_colors) +
  labs(title = "Log(UIC) by Urbanization",
       x = "Area Type", y = "Log(UIC)") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none")

print(p6 / (p7 | p8))
```

```{r uic-category-by-urban}
# WHO category distribution stacked bar by urbanicity
uic_categories %>%
  mutate(area_type = factor(ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural"))) %>%
  count(area_type, uic_category) %>%
  group_by(area_type) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = area_type, y = percent, fill = uic_category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percent)),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),  # ggplot handles midpoint automatically
    color = "white",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(title = "UIC Category Distribution by Urban/Rural Status",
       x = "Area Type", y = "Percentage (%)", fill = "UIC Category") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## 3. Regional Patterns

```{r regional-summary}
# Mean UIC and urbanization rate by region
regional_summary <- uic_data %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw),
    median_uic = median(uic_raw),
    sd_uic     = sd(uic_raw),
    pct_urban  = mean(urban) * 100,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_uic))

regional_summary %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(n), ~round(., 2))) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "UIC by Region")
```

```{r regional-plots, fig.height=6}
# Mean UIC by region (colored by % urban) and boxplot by region
p10 <- regional_summary %>%
  mutate(region = fct_reorder(factor(region), mean_uic)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = region, y = mean_uic, fill = pct_urban)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Mean UIC by Region",
       subtitle = "Color indicates urbanization percentage",
       x = "Region", y = "Mean UIC (\u03bcg/L)", fill = "% Urban") +
  theme_minimal()

p11 <- uic_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = region, y = log_uic, fill = region)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(title = "Log(UIC) Distribution by Region",
       x = "Region", y = "Log(UIC)") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none")

print(p10)
print(p11)
```

```{r urban-rural-by-region}
# Urban-rural UIC comparison within each region
regional_urban_rural <- uic_data %>%
  mutate(area_type = ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural")) %>%
  group_by(region, area_type) %>%
  summarise(n          = n(),
            mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw),
            median_uic = median(uic_raw),
            .groups    = "drop")

regional_urban_rural %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from  = area_type,
              values_from = c(n, mean_uic, median_uic),
              names_sep   = "_") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_uic_Urban)) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !starts_with("n_"), ~round(., 2))) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "Urban-Rural UIC by Region")
```

```{r urban-rural-region-plot}
# Dodged bar chart: mean UIC by region and area type
regional_urban_rural %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = region, y = mean_uic, fill = area_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Rural" = "#E69F00", "Urban" = "#56B4E9")) +
  labs(title = "Mean UIC by Region and Urbanization",
       x = "Region", y = "Mean UIC (\u03bcg/L)", fill = "Area Type") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## 4. Temporal Patterns (Survey Year)

```{r temporal-summary}
# UIC summary by survey year
uic_data %>%
  group_by(survey_year) %>%
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw),
    median_uic = median(uic_raw),
    sd_uic     = sd(uic_raw),
    pct_urban  = mean(urban) * 100,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !c(survey_year, n), ~round(., 2))) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "UIC by Survey Year")

```

```{r temporal-plot}
# Boxplot of log(UIC) by survey year and urbanicity
uic_data %>%
  mutate(area_type = factor(ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(survey_year), y = log_uic, fill = area_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Rural" = "#E69F00", "Urban" = "#56B4E9")) +
  labs(title = "Log(UIC) by Survey Year and Urbanization",
       x = "Survey Year", y = "Log(UIC)", fill = "Area Type") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## 5. Demographic Patterns

```{r age-group-uic}
# UIC by age group
uic_age_data <- uic_data %>%
  mutate(
    age_group = cut(age,
                    breaks = c(0, 5, 12, 18, 30, 50, 70, Inf),
                    labels = c("0-5", "6-12", "13-18",
                               "19-30", "31-50", "51-70", "70+"),
                    right  = FALSE)
  )

uic_age_data %>%
  group_by(age_group) %>%
  summarise(n          = n(),
            mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw),
            median_uic = median(uic_raw),
            .groups    = "drop") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !n, ~round(., 2))) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "UIC by Age Group")
```

```{r age-group-plot}
ggplot(uic_age_data, aes(x = age_group, y = log_uic, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(title = "Log(UIC) by Age Group",
       x = "Age Group (years)", y = "Log(UIC)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
```

```{r sex-uic}
# UIC by sex
uic_data %>%
  group_by(sex) %>%
  summarise(n          = n(),
            mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw),
            median_uic = median(uic_raw),
            sd_uic     = sd(uic_raw),
            .groups    = "drop") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !n, ~round(., 2))) %>%
  kable(format = "simple", caption = "UIC by Sex")
```

```{r sex-ttest}
# Two-sample t-test: UIC by sex
sex_t <- t.test(uic_raw ~ sex, data = uic_data)
cat("T-test by Sex:",
    "\n  t =", round(sex_t$statistic, 3),
    "\n  p =", format.pval(sex_t$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")

t.test(log_uic ~ sex, data = uic_data)
```

```{r sex-plot}
ggplot(uic_data, aes(x = sex, y = log_uic, fill = sex)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Male" = "#00BFC4", "Female" = "#F8766D")) +
  labs(title = "Log(UIC) by Sex", x = "Sex", y = "Log(UIC)") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r education-by-urban}
# Education level by urbanicity (key confounder in adjustment set)
analysis_data |>
  filter(!is.na(education)) |>
  mutate(area_type = ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural")) |>
  group_by(area_type) |>
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    mean_educ   = mean(education),
    median_educ = median(education),
    sd_educ     = sd(education),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !n, ~round(., 2))) |>
  kable(format = "simple",
        caption = "Education Level by Urban/Rural Status")
```

## 6. Inflammation Proxy Assessment

```{r proxy-by-urban}
# Hemoglobin and serum retinol stratified by urbanicity
# Establishes that proxies vary by exposure, supporting their use in CEVAE
analysis_data |>
  filter(!is.na(hemoglobin), !is.na(serum_retinol)) |>
  mutate(area_type = ifelse(urban == 1, "Urban", "Rural")) |>
  group_by(area_type) |>
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    mean_hb   = mean(hemoglobin),
    sd_hb     = sd(hemoglobin),
    mean_vita = mean(serum_retinol),
    sd_vita   = sd(serum_retinol),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !n, ~round(., 2))) |>
  kable(format = "simple",
        caption = "Inflammation Proxies by Urban/Rural Status")
```

```{r proxy-correlations}
# Marginal correlations between proxies and outcome
hb_data <- uic_data %>% filter(!is.na(hemoglobin))
sr_data <- uic_data %>% filter(!is.na(serum_retinol))

cat("Hemoglobin correlations:\n")
cat("  UIC vs Hb:     ", round(cor(hb_data$uic_raw,  hb_data$hemoglobin), 3), "\n")
cat("  Log(UIC) vs Hb:", round(cor(hb_data$log_uic,  hb_data$hemoglobin), 3), "\n")

cat("\nSerum Retinol correlations:\n")
cat("  UIC vs SR:     ", round(cor(sr_data$uic_raw,  sr_data$serum_retinol), 3), "\n")
cat("  Log(UIC) vs SR:", round(cor(sr_data$log_uic,  sr_data$serum_retinol), 3), "\n")
```

```{r proxy-scatter-plots}
# Scatter plots: proxies vs log(UIC)
p17 <- ggplot(hb_data, aes(x = hemoglobin, y = log_uic)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Hemoglobin vs Log(UIC)",
       x = "Hemoglobin (g/dL)", y = "Log(UIC)") +
  theme_minimal()

p18 <- ggplot(sr_data, aes(x = serum_retinol, y = log_uic)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "coral") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "darkred", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Serum Retinol vs Log(UIC)",
       x = "Serum Retinol (\u03bcg/dL)", y = "Log(UIC)") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p17 | p18)
```

```{r correlation-matrix}
# Correlation matrix: outcome, proxies, and age
inflammation_data <- uic_data %>%
  select(log_uic, hemoglobin, serum_retinol, age) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))

corrplot(cor(inflammation_data),
         method       = "color",
         type         = "upper",
         addCoef.col  = "black",
         tl.col       = "black",
         title        = "Correlation Matrix: Outcome and Inflammation Proxies",
         mar          = c(0, 0, 2, 0))
```

## 7. Complete-Case Sample Comparison

```{r complete-case-prep}
# Prepare complete-case dataset (used in DAG testing and downstream stages)
df_test <- analysis_data |>
  dplyr::select(log_uic, urban, hemoglobin, serum_retinol,
         age, sex, education, region) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    sex    = as.numeric(as.factor(sex)),
    region = as.numeric(as.factor(region)),
    educ   = as.numeric(education)
  ) |>
  na.omit()

cat("Complete-case sample size:", nrow(df_test), "\n")
```

```{r complete-case-comparison}
# Compare complete-case sample against full dataset on key variables
# Checks whether listwise deletion introduces systematic bias
full_summary <- analysis_data |>
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_uic   = mean(uic_raw,  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_age   = mean(age,      na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_urban  = mean(urban,    na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    pct_female = mean(sex == "Female", na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) |> mutate(sample = "Full dataset")

cc_summary <- df_test |>
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_uic   = mean(exp(log_uic)),
    mean_age   = mean(age),
    pct_urban  = mean(urban) * 100,
    pct_female = mean(sex == 2) * 100   # Female = level 2 after as.numeric()
  ) |> mutate(sample = "Complete-case")

bind_rows(full_summary, cc_summary) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !n, ~round(., 2))) |>
  select(sample, everything()) |>
  kable(format = "simple",
        caption = "Full Dataset vs Complete-Case Sample Comparison")
```

## 8. DAG Validation

```{r dag-final-define}
dag_final <- dagitty('dag {
  urban       -> log_uic
  urban       -> hemoglobin
  urban       -> vita

  Z_latent -> log_uic
  Z_latent -> hemoglobin
  Z_latent -> vita

  age  -> log_uic
  age  -> urban
  age  -> Z_latent
  age  -> educ
  age  -> region

  sex  -> log_uic
  sex  -> Z_latent
  sex  -> hemoglobin
  sex  -> educ
  sex  -> urban
  sex  -> age

  educ -> urban
  educ -> log_uic
  educ -> vita
  educ -> hemoglobin

  region -> urban
  region -> log_uic
  region -> Z_latent
}')

plot(dag_final)
```

```{r dag-final-independencies}
# Implied conditional independencies from dag_final
impliedConditionalIndependencies(dag_final)

paths(dag_final, from = "urban", to = "log_uic")
```

```{r dag-local-tests}
# Local independence testing on 5000-observation complete-case sample
latents(dag_final) <- "Z_latent"

set.seed(0942)
df_sample <- df_test |> sample_n(5000)

results_final <- localTests(dag_final,
                             data = df_sample,
                             type = "cis",
                             R    = 200)

results_final |>
  arrange(desc(abs(estimate))) |>
  print()
```

```{r dag-local-test-plot}
plotLocalTestResults(results_final)
```

```{r adjustment-sets}
# Minimally sufficient adjustment sets for causal effect of urban -> log_uic
adjustmentSets(dag_final,
               exposure = "urban",
               outcome  = "log_uic",
               effect   = "total")

adjustmentSets(dag_final,
               exposure = "urban",
               outcome  = "log_uic",
               effect   = "direct")
```
